meaning: adj.起皱的,绉褶多的,卷缩的
examples: His back ached badly, and he noticed with dismay that his paws were beginning to get all crinkly.
His crinkly hair was touched with grey…
Look at these crinkly red petals. I like them so much.
It was the need to fit in more columns that forced the human brain into its crinkly, wrinkled shape.
Tony read the letter over breakfast and shook his copy noisily at me newsprint was crinkly and loud back then.
